title: ipomopsis spicata subsp. spicata
intro: Ipomopsis spicata subsp. spicata, also known as the Spiked Globe Mallow or Scarlet Gilia, is a perennial wildflower native to the western United States and Canada. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to treat various health issues, including wounds, skin irritations, and respiratory problems. The health benefits of Ipomopsis spicata subsp. spicata include its ability to reduce inflammation, fight infections, and promote healing, making it a valuable herbal remedy for various ailments. Ipomopsis spicata subsp. spicata can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and can also be used topically as a salve or poultice to apply directly to affected areas.
